title: Down Payment Calculator for Home Buyers
shortDescription: Calculate your home down payment and remaining loan amount instantly.
description: Enter a home price and down payment percentage to see your down payment amount and the loan you will need. A simple tool for home buyers planning a mortgage.
metaDescription: Free down payment calculator for home buyers. Enter the home price and percentage to instantly find your down payment and the remaining mortgage loan amount.

result.empty: Enter a home price and a down payment percentage to see your results.

sections.heading: What is a down payment?
paragraphs: A down payment is the portion of a home's price you pay upfront in cash. The rest is covered by a mortgage loan.
This calculator multiplies the price by your chosen percentage to find the down payment, then subtracts it from the price to show the loan you still need.

sections.heading: Why your down payment matters
paragraphs: A larger down payment lowers your loan amount, reduces monthly payments, and can help you avoid extra mortgage insurance costs.
Many buyers aim for 20%, but the right percentage depends on your savings, budget, and lender requirements.

howTo.name: Enter the home price
howTo.text: Type the full purchase price of the home you want to buy.

howTo.name: Enter the down payment percent
howTo.text: Type the percentage of the price you plan to pay upfront, such as 20.

howTo.name: Read your results
howTo.text: The tool shows your down payment amount and the remaining loan you will need.

faq.q: How is the down payment calculated?
faq.a: The down payment equals the home price multiplied by your chosen percentage, divided by 100.

faq.q: How is the loan amount found?
faq.a: The loan amount is the home price minus your down payment.

faq.q: How much down payment do I need?
faq.a: It varies by lender, but 20% is a common target to avoid extra insurance. Some loans allow far less.

faq.q: Does a bigger down payment help?
faq.a: Yes. It lowers your loan, reduces monthly payments, and may cut interest and insurance costs.

faq.q: Does this include taxes or fees?
faq.a: No. It calculates only the down payment and the base loan amount, not closing costs or other fees.
